title: Fluffy Middle Eastern Flour Flatbread
description: A fresh, fluffy plant‑based flatbread elevated with aromatic Sichuan pepper, perfect for a quick sick‑day meal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 220 °C (425 °F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Whisk the flour, salt, and sugar in a large bowl. In a separate container, dissolve yeast in warm water, then let sit for 5 min until foamy.
Add the yeast mixture and olive oil to the dry ingredients. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 6 min until smooth and elastic.
Return dough to the bowl, cover with a damp towel, and let rise in a warm spot for 45 min or until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough, divide it into 6 equal portions, and roll each into a circle about ¼‑inch thick.
Place the flatbreads on the prepared sheet, brush the tops with a little olive oil, and sprinkle crushed Sichuan peppercorns evenly over each.
If using, sprinkle shredded plant‑based cheese on top of each flatbread.
Bake for 12–15 min until golden and puffed. Remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Optional: garnish with chopped dill before serving.
